Visual Research
📸 Payment Solutions & Flow Screenshots
Each card shows a real checkout flow captured during R&D testing. Screenshots are cropped from research slides. Russian annotations have been translated into English and placed as card notes.
🏦
Providers with Multiple Solutions
Merchants using providers that cover multiple payment methods
bc.game — Nequi + Bre-B
🏪 bc.game 💳 Nequi, Bre-B 🔗 paydeskco.brcashypro.com → checkout.co.1pago.net
bc.game checkout showing Nequi and Bre-B deposit options, both routed through Cashy / 1pago. The Bre-B path displays a unique llave (key) and timer for completing the bank transfer.
⚠ Pay Attention
Nequi is implemented through checkout.co.1pago.net. The flow redirects users from paydeskco.brcashypro.com to 1pago.
betwinner — Paycash Bre-B
🏪 betwinner 💳 Paycash, Bre-B 🔗 Paycash (in-store and digital wallets)
betwinner deposit page listing Paycash, Davivienda, Tpaga, and Bre-B. On the Bre-B route, Paycash shows a QR code with a 40-second timer and refácil network integration.
betwinner — Davivienda via Paycash
🏪 betwinner 💳 Davivienda (via Paycash) 🔗 Paycash
betwinner deposit with Davivienda digital payment option highlighted. Paycash then shows payment instructions for completing the transfer via Davivienda online banking.
betwinner — Tpaga QR via Paycash
🏪 betwinner 💳 Tpaga QR (via Paycash) 🔗 Paycash
betwinner deposit selecting Tpaga through Paycash. The provider page asks for a mobile number and then generates a QR code for scanning in the Tpaga app.
🏛️
PSE Flows
Pago Seguro en Línea — bank-based online transfers observed across merchants
PSE — Providers Overview
🏪 1win, bc.game, betwinner, others 💳 PSE 🔗 4bill, co-payer.erjqport.com, checkout.smartfastpay.com, h5.supefina.net, pay.trx-point.com, pro.h5.starpago.com, payretailers.com
Overview of PSE providers observed during testing. Six distinct routes were identified across merchants, covering different form fields and redirect patterns.
1win / bc.game — PSE via 4bill (Cobre)
🏪 1win, bc.game 💳 PSE 🔗 4bill → Cobre
1win displays a full merchant-side PSE form (name, ID type, document number, phone, email) before redirecting to 4bill. bc.game shows a minimal form — bank selection and personal info are required on the provider (Cobre) side.
⚠ Pay Attention
1win asks for user details on the merchant side. bc.game does not ask on the merchant side, but the provider form (4bill/Cobre) requires them.
bc.game / 20bet — PSE via co-payer.erjqport.com
🏪 bc.game, 20bet 💳 PSE 🔗 co-payer.erjqport.com
bc.game deposit with PSE leading to the co-payer.erjqport.com form. It collects bank, identity type, ID, phone, and email. Merchant pre-filled user data on the provider form.
⚠ Pay Attention
The merchant passed user data to the provider. On the merchant side, bc.game and 20bet ask only for the ID number.
betwinner — PSE via checkout.smartfastpay.com
🏪 betwinner 💳 PSE 🔗 checkout.smartfastpay.com
betwinner PSE through SmartFastPay. The merchant collects full personal data (name, email, phone, document type, number) before redirecting to SmartFastPay which shows an additional bank-selection step.
bc.game — PSE via h5.supefina.net
🏪 bc.game 💳 PSE 🔗 h5.supefina.net
bc.game PSE routed through Supefina. The bc.game form asks for email, ID, and phone. The Supefina page shows bank and personal data fields — NEQUI appears as a bank option in the dropdown.
melbet / pocketoption — PSE via pay.trx-point.com (Flow 1)
🏪 melbet, pocketoption 💳 PSE 🔗 pay.trx-point.com
PSE flow 1 through TRX Point. The merchant form collects name, surname, email, phone, and CC/NIT. On the provider side, only a bank selection field is shown — no additional personal data fields.
⚠ Pay Attention
On the provider side, only the bank selection is required in this flow.
melbet / others — PSE via pay.trx-point.com (Flow 2)
🏪 melbet, others 💳 PSE 🔗 pay.trx-point.com
PSE flow 2 through TRX Point. Unlike flow 1, the provider side shows a phone number field in addition to the bank selector. Phone number appears mandatory in this variant.
⚠ Pay Attention
Phone number appears to be required in this PSE flow where it is requested by the provider.
bc.game — PSE via payretailers.com
🏪 bc.game 💳 PSE 🔗 payretailers.com
bc.game PSE deposit through PayRetailers (0% commission, LATAM Banking). The PayRetailers form collects bank, client type, name, document number, telephone, and email.
PSE — topup.com.co / tumipay.com screenshot
🏪 Jugabet, bc.game 💳 PSE 🔗 co-payer.erjqport.com → topup.com.co / pro.h5.starpago.com → topup.com.co
PSE payment page where the intermediate provider redirected to the Tumi Pay checkout (topup.com.co). The form shows PSE bank and contact detail fields with a time limit for completing the payment.
⚠ Pay Attention
In two cases, providers redirected users to the PSP tumipay.com (topup.com.co).
Jugabet — PSE via co-payer.erjqport.com → topup.com.co
🏪 Jugabet 💳 PSE 🔗 co-payer.erjqport.com → topup.com.co (Tumi Pay)
Jugabet PSE starting at co-payer.erjqport.com. The co-payer form collects bank, identity type, ID, phone, and email. The final step lands on the Tumi Pay (topup.com.co) PSE checkout with bank and contact fields.
⚠ Pay Attention
topup.com.co is provided by Tumi Pay.
bc.game — PSE via pro.h5.starpago.com → topup.com.co
🏪 bc.game 💳 PSE 🔗 pro.h5.starpago.com → topup.com.co (Tumi Pay)
bc.game PSE starting at starpago.com. After the intermediate provider collects personal data, the user is redirected to the Tumi Pay (topup.com.co) PSE checkout with bank and contact fields.
⚠ Pay Attention
topup.com.co is provided by Tumi Pay.
📱
Nequi Flows
Nequi push, H2H, and P2P deposit flows
1win — Nequi Push via 4bill
🏪 1win 💳 Nequi Push 🔗 4bill
1win Nequi Push through 4bill. The merchant shows Nequi PSE, Nequi Push, and Nequi options. After collecting name, phone, and email, a push notification is sent to the user's Nequi app with a 15-minute confirmation window.
Jugabet — Nequi Push via 4bill / Payzen
🏪 Jugabet 💳 Nequi Push 🔗 4bill → Payzen (via Cobre)
Jugabet Nequi Push through 4bill / Payzen. After selecting Nequi Push, the Payzen page shows a QR code with a Nequi logo and a countdown timer for scanning in the Nequi app.
1win / 1xbet / Jugabet / Melbet / Binomo — Nequi Push H2H
🏪 1win, 1xbet, Jugabet, Melbet, Binomo 💳 Nequi Push (H2H) 🔗 Direct H2H integration (no external provider redirect)
H2H Nequi Push flow used by multiple merchants. The form asks for document type, document number, phone, and amount. The user confirms the push in their Nequi app within 15 minutes. No redirect to an external provider.
⚠ Pay Attention
Nequi push H2H works without redirect to an external provider.
Various — Nequi Push via TRX Point
🏪 Various 💳 Nequi Push 🔗 pay.trx-point.com
Nequi Push through TRX Point. Merchant collects name, email, phone, and ID. The TRX Point provider page asks for document type and document number, and confirms that a payment request will be sent to the Nequi app.
bc.game — Nequi via co-payer.erjqport.com → 1pago
🏪 bc.game 💳 Nequi 🔗 co-payer.erjqport.com → checkout.co.1pago.net (1PAGO)
bc.game Nequi flow starting at co-payer.erjqport.com. The form collects identity type, ID, and phone. The merchant passed user data to the provider. The final step redirects to the 1PAGO Nequi checkout.
⚠ Pay Attention
The merchant passed user data to the provider. The flow redirects to 1pago for the final Nequi confirmation step.
1xbet — Nequi P2P
🏪 1xbet 💳 Nequi P2P 🔗 Direct P2P
1xbet Nequi P2P deposit. The form shows a Nequi phone number and account holder name. The user manually transfers the exact amount in their Nequi app, then fills in their phone number, account name, and uploads a screenshot as proof.
⚠ Pay Attention
1xbet Nequi P2P is the only P2P Nequi solution observed in the market.
🔗
Bre-B Flows
Bre-B interoperable transfer flows
1win — Bre-B (H2H / P2P)
🏪 1win 💳 Bre-B 🔗 Direct H2H / P2P
1win Bre-B with two variants: H2H (left) shows amount entry before deposit confirmation; P2P (right) provides a unique Bre-B llave (key) and a countdown timer for the bank transfer.
Binomo — Bre-B via topup.com.co (Tumi Pay)
🏪 Binomo 💳 Bre-B 🔗 topup.com.co (Tumi Pay)
Binomo Bre-B routed through Tumi Pay (topup.com.co). The deposit selector shows Bre-B. The Tumi Pay page provides a unique Bre-B llave with a 15-minute countdown and step-by-step bank app instructions.
⚠ Pay Attention
topup.com.co is provided by Tumi Pay.
Jugabet — Bre-B via 4bill (Cobre)
🏪 Jugabet 💳 Bre-B 🔗 4bill → Cobre
Jugabet Bre-B through 4bill / Cobre. The form offers Bre-B Llave and QR options. The Cobre page shows a unique Bre-B key, amount, and payment confirmation instructions.
👥
P2P / QR / Wallet Flows
Peer-to-peer transfers, QR codes, and digital wallet deposits
1xbet — Bancolombia P2P
🏪 1xbet 💳 Bancolombia P2P 🔗 Direct P2P
1xbet Bancolombia P2P. Shows recipient's full name, NIT, and phone number. User enters account number, name, and phone, then uploads a screenshot of the completed bank transfer.
betwinner — Bancolombia P2P
🏪 betwinner 💳 Bancolombia P2P 🔗 Direct P2P (BW Payment Gate)
betwinner Bancolombia P2P via BW Payment Gate. Step 1 shows bank account details (name, NIT, account number, account type). Step 2 collects the deposit amount, player account number, player name, transaction ID, and payment screenshot.
1xbet — Daviplata via api.lamapay.org
🏪 1xbet 💳 Daviplata 🔗 api.lamapay.org (Lamapay)
1xbet Daviplata through the Lamapay API. The provider page shows the recipient's phone number and name for a Daviplata transfer, plus a 'Ya pagué' (I already paid) button to confirm completion.
1xbet — Daviplata P2P
🏪 1xbet 💳 Daviplata P2P 🔗 Direct P2P
1xbet Daviplata P2P deposit. Shows the recipient's Daviplata phone number and account name. User enters their own account details, uploads a screenshot, and must write TRANSFER in the transaction comment.
1win — Tpaga QR
🏪 1win 💳 Tpaga QR 🔗 Direct Tpaga H2H
1win Tpaga QR deposit. The merchant form collects document type, document number, and amount. After submission, the provider generates a QR code with a Download QR button for scanning in the Tpaga app.
melbet — Tpaga QR
🏪 melbet 💳 Tpaga QR 🔗 Direct Tpaga integration
melbet Tpaga QR deposit. Merchant form collects name, email, phone, document type, and document number. The provider returns a large QR code and a tpaga URL with instructions to open the Tpaga app and scan.
Binomo — Tpaga via checkout.pagsmile.com
🏪 Binomo 💳 Tpaga QR 🔗 checkout.pagsmile.com (Pagsmile)
Binomo Tpaga routed through Pagsmile checkout. Pagsmile presents a QR code with instructions to select Colombia → Tpaga, then scan the code in the Tpaga app to complete the payment.

Payment Method Findings
Field analysis per payment method — based only on observed data. Helps identify the minimum field set for each flow.
Legend: Core appears in all or almost all flows Common appears in many but not all flows Additional provider-specific or flow-specific only
🏦 PSE
Core
Full name Document number Email
Common
Phone number Document type Bank
Additional
Address City Account type
Merchant side pattern

Most PSE merchant forms request Full name + one identity field (Document number or Email). The 4bill-mediated merchants (bc.game, Brazino 777) show empty merchant sides — they delegate entirely to the provider. Direct integration merchants (1xbet, 20bet) ask for Bank and document fields themselves.

Provider side pattern

Provider APIs consistently require Bank + identity fields (Full name, Document type, Document number, Email, Phone number). This set appears in supefina.net, smartfastpay.com, and starpago flows. Simpler providers (4bill) only ask for Full name + Phone number + Email.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Bank Full name Email Document type Document number
To build a low-friction PSE flow, collect Bank, Full name, Email, Document type, and Document number. Bank selection is essential — PSE routes the payment based on the user's bank. Phone number and Address should be added only if the specific PSP requires them. City appears provider-specific and is not a core field.
📱 Nequi
Core
Full name Phone number Document number
Common
Document type Email
Additional
Screenshot Comment
Merchant side pattern

Direct push flows (without PSP) uniformly collect identity + phone data. The 4bill-mediated routes show minimal or no merchant-side fields, with 4bill's provider API always requesting Full name + Phone number + Email. The Nequi p2p route is a manual flow requiring screenshots — not scalable.

Provider side pattern

4bill and 4bill Payzen (QR) consistently ask for Full name + Phone number + Email via their API. TRX point asks for Document type (and sometimes Document number) on the provider side. Since Nequi is phone-linked, Phone number appears critical at the API layer.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Full name Phone number Document type Document number
For Nequi, Phone number is the core identifier — the wallet is phone-linked. Full name, Document type, and Document number appear consistently across flows. Email is useful but not universal. Avoid screenshot-based flows in production.
💳 Bre-b
Core
Full name Phone number
Additional
Email provider API level only
Merchant side pattern

Bre-b data is sparse. Most merchant-side cells are empty, suggesting either the merchant collects data before hand-off or the flow is redirect-based. The only filled merchant form (Jugabet via 4bill→cobre) shows Full name + Phone number.

Provider side pattern

The 4bill→cobre provider consistently requests Full name + Phone number + Email at the API level. topup.com.co shows no observed provider-side fields — data may be missing or the flow is simpler.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Full name Phone number Email
Based on limited data, Full name + Phone number appear to be the core fields for Bre-b merchant flows. Email is likely needed at the provider API level. Data coverage for this method is thin — treat these recommendations with caution.
🏛️ Bancolombia
Core
Full name
Common
Phone number Email Document number
Additional
Document type Bank number Screenshot TX id
Merchant side pattern

4bill-mediated merchants (1win, Jugabet, Brazino777) show minimal or empty merchant sides. PSP-integrated providers (supefina, smartfastpay) request a richer set. P2P routes require manual proof (bank account number, screenshot) — not suitable for automated flows.

Provider side pattern

4bill API asks for Full name + Phone number + Email. supefina.net and smartfastpay.com APIs ask for Full name + Document type + Document number + Email + Phone number — a richer set. P2P has no observed provider-side API.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Full name Phone number Email
For Bancolombia via 4bill (the most common observed provider), Full name + Phone number + Email appears sufficient. If using supefina.net or smartfastpay.com, add Document type and Document number. Avoid P2P flows for automated processing.
📲 Daviplata
Core
Full name
Common
Phone number
Additional
Email Document type Document number Address Screenshot
Merchant side pattern

4bill for Daviplata shows an empty merchant side — consistent with its pattern of delegating to its own API. Direct PSE integration (melbet) requests a full identity set. api.lamapay.org/ p2p requests only Full name; the standalone P2P route for 1xbet requests Full name + Phone + Screenshot — a manual verification flow.

Provider side pattern

4bill API requests Full name + Phone number + Email. checkout.pagsmile.com requests a full identity set including Address. P2P and Direct PSE entries show no provider-side API data, suggesting direct bank-side integration.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Full name Phone number
Daviplata is phone-linked — Phone number is the core identifier. Full name appears in all non-empty flows. Document fields and Address are provider-specific (Direct PSE / pagsmile). Start with Full name + Phone number and extend based on the provider chosen.
📷 Tpaga (QR)
Core
Document type Document number
Additional
Full name Email Phone number Address
Merchant side pattern

Very limited data — only P2P-routed merchants (1win and melbet) have observed merchant-side fields. 1win shows a minimal set (Document type + Document number), while melbet's form is more complete. checkout.pagsmile.com / Binomo shows no fields on either side.

Provider side pattern

No provider-side fields observed in this payment method. checkout.pagsmile.com shows both sides empty for Tpaga (QR), which may indicate incomplete data capture at research time.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Document type Document number
Based on limited data, Document type and Document number appear consistently for QR-based flows. Tpaga (QR) data coverage is very thin — treat this recommendation with caution and verify directly with the PSP before building.
🔄 Movii (PSE)
Core
Full name Email Phone number
Additional
Document type Document number Address
Merchant side pattern

Only 2 merchant rows observed. The 4bill route shows no merchant-side fields. The Direct PSE route (melbet) shows a full identity set including Address. Movii (PSE) appears to behave similarly to standard PSE in terms of required data.

Provider side pattern

Only 4bill shows provider-side data: Full name + Phone number + Email. Consistent with 4bill's standard API requirement across all payment methods.

Recommended Minimum Field Set
Full name Email Phone number
Extremely limited data — only 2 providers and 2 merchants observed. Treat PSE field requirements as a baseline, then adjust based on provider. The 4bill API set (Full name + Phone + Email) appears as the practical minimum for this method.
Colombia R&D · Payment Providers & Fields · May 2026
